pairs.aText: Do you trust your first instinct?
pairs.bText: Did your gut tell you something today that you ignored?
pairs.note: You trust your first instinct, yet today your gut spoke and went unheard.

pairs.aText: Are you being honest with yourself?
pairs.bText: Have you told yourself a comforting story that isn't quite true?
pairs.note: You feel honest with yourself, yet a comforting story is still being told.

pairs.aText: Do you rest without guilt when you need to?
pairs.bText: Have you put off resting because there's always more to do?
pairs.note: You say rest comes without guilt, yet it keeps getting postponed for the to-do list.

pairs.aText: Do you rest before you're forced to?
pairs.bText: Did you push through exhaustion when you should have stopped?
pairs.note: You rest before you're forced to, you say, yet today exhaustion had to push first.

pairs.aText: Are you living with intention?
pairs.bText: Do you say yes out of habit?
pairs.note: You're living with intention, yet yes still slips out by habit.

pairs.aText: Can you be still without feeling guilty?
pairs.bText: Did you rest without guilt?
pairs.note: You believe stillness can be guilt-free, yet guilt followed your rest today.

pairs.aText: Do you take breaks before you burn out, not after?
pairs.bText: Have you been borrowing energy from tomorrow to get through today?
pairs.note: You pause before burning out, you say, yet tomorrow keeps lending you energy.

pairs.aText: Did you let yourself enjoy something without earning it first?
pairs.bText: Did you skip self-care this week because you felt you hadn't earned it?
pairs.note: Part of you enjoys without earning, while another part still waits for permission.

pairs.aText: Would you defend your free time like you defend a meeting?
pairs.bText: Have you protected your time this week?
pairs.note: You'd defend your free time like a meeting, yet this week it went unguarded.

pairs.aText: Can you name the thing that's been draining you?
pairs.bText: Have you said no to something that drains you?
pairs.note: You can name what drains you, yet the no hasn't been said.

pairs.aText: Have you been gentle with yourself lately?
pairs.bText: Would you be friends with someone who spoke to you the way you speak to yourself?
pairs.note: You feel gentle with yourself, yet you wouldn't befriend your own inner voice.

pairs.aText: Would you forgive a friend for the things you blame yourself for?
pairs.bText: Is there something you need to forgive yourself for?
pairs.note: A friend would already be forgiven, yet you're still waiting on your own pardon.

pairs.aText: Do you coach yourself more than you criticize yourself?
pairs.bText: Did you speak to yourself kindly after a mistake today?
pairs.note: You see yourself as your own coach, yet today's mistake met a harsher voice.

pairs.aText: Can you hear a hard truth without flinching?
pairs.bText: Is there feedback you've been avoiding hearing?
pairs.note: You can take a hard truth, yet one piece of feedback still waits at the door.

pairs.aText: Can you ask for honest feedback on your work?
pairs.bText: Is there feedback you've been avoiding hearing?
pairs.note: You can ask for honest feedback, yet some of it you've been keeping at a distance.

pairs.aText: Do you soften your opinions to keep the peace?
pairs.bText: Did you say what you actually meant today?
pairs.note: You tend to soften your opinions, yet today your real words found their way out.

pairs.aText: Did you listen to your intuition recently?
pairs.bText: Is there a quiet knowing you've been talking yourself out of?
pairs.note: You listen to your intuition, yet one quiet knowing keeps getting talked down.

pairs.aText: Do you trust yourself to figure things out?
pairs.bText: Are you waiting to feel confident before you act?
pairs.note: You trust yourself to figure things out, yet you're waiting for confidence to go first.

pairs.aText: Would you still choose the safe path if no one was watching?
pairs.bText: Is fear making a decision for you right now?
pairs.note: Unwatched, you wouldn't choose the safe path, yet fear is quietly choosing for you.

pairs.aText: Do you believe you can get better at hard things?
pairs.bText: Are you avoiding a task because it might reveal your limits?
pairs.note: You believe you can grow through hard things, yet one task stays untouched in case it proves otherwise.

pairs.aText: Do you choose courage in small doses every day?
pairs.bText: Is there a conversation you keep rehearsing but never having?
pairs.note: You choose small daily courage, yet one conversation stays in rehearsal.

pairs.aText: Would you rather try and stumble than always wonder?
pairs.bText: Is there a leap you keep walking up to the edge of?
pairs.note: You'd rather stumble than wonder, yet one leap keeps you at its edge.

pairs.aText: Do you let yourself be a beginner without apology?
pairs.bText: Are you afraid of looking like a beginner?
pairs.note: You let yourself begin without apology, yet looking like a beginner still feels frightening.

pairs.aText: Can you enjoy making something you're not good at yet?
pairs.bText: Are you okay being average at something while you learn?
pairs.note: You enjoy making things you haven't mastered, yet being average while learning still sits uneasily.

pairs.aText: Is creating part of who you are?
pairs.bText: Do you protect time for creating?
pairs.note: Creating is part of who you are, yet its time on the calendar goes unprotected.

pairs.aText: Do you let yourself write bad first drafts?
pairs.bText: Is perfectionism quieting something you want to make?
pairs.note: You allow rough first drafts, yet perfectionism is still hushing something you want to make.

pairs.aText: Would you ship the imperfect thing tomorrow?
pairs.bText: Is perfectionism quieting something you want to make?
pairs.note: You'd ship the imperfect thing tomorrow, yet perfectionism keeps something of yours quiet today.

pairs.aText: Do you trust your own taste?
pairs.bText: Do you compare your work to others' more than you'd like?
pairs.note: You trust your own taste, yet other people's work keeps becoming the measuring stick.

pairs.aText: Do you believe everyone is creative?
pairs.bText: Do you think of yourself as a creative person?
pairs.note: You believe everyone is creative, yet somehow that everyone doesn't include you.

pairs.aText: Do you practice anything creative regularly?
pairs.bText: Would you call yourself an artist out loud?
pairs.note: You practice your craft regularly, yet the word artist stays unspoken.

pairs.aText: Do you wish you played make-believe more often?
pairs.bText: Did you give yourself permission to play today?
pairs.note: You wish for more play, yet today's permission slip went unsigned.

pairs.aText: Have you listened to your body's signals?
pairs.bText: Did you push through pain you should have listened to?
pairs.note: You listen to your body, yet today its pain was pushed through rather than heard.

pairs.aText: Have you been honest with yourself about how tired you are?
pairs.bText: Did you stay up too late doing nothing in particular?
pairs.note: You know how tired you are, yet the late hours still drifted by unclaimed.

pairs.aText: Have you set any limits on your screen time?
pairs.bText: Did you lose an hour to scrolling without meaning to?
pairs.note: You've set limits on your screens, yet an hour still slipped through the scroll.

pairs.aText: Have you noticed how you feel after a long scroll?
pairs.bText: Did you reach for your phone the moment you felt bored today?
pairs.note: You know how the long scroll leaves you feeling, yet boredom still reaches for the phone.

pairs.aText: Can you sit with yourself without reaching for a distraction?
pairs.bText: Do you reach for your phone the second you feel uneasy?
pairs.note: You can sit with yourself, you say, yet unease still sends your hand to the phone.

pairs.aText: Have you figured out what reliably recharges you?
pairs.bText: Have you been running on empty lately?
pairs.note: You know what recharges you, yet lately the tank stays empty.

pairs.aText: Do you know what matters most to you right now?
pairs.bText: Is your time going where you say it matters?
pairs.note: You know what matters most, yet your hours are traveling somewhere else.

pairs.aText: Did you have a productive day?
pairs.bText: Is your busyness building anything?
pairs.note: Today felt productive, yet the busyness doesn't seem to be building anything.

pairs.aText: Does knowing life is short make you gentler?
pairs.bText: Have you told the people who matter what they mean to you?
pairs.note: Life's shortness softens you, yet the people who matter haven't heard it from you.

pairs.aText: Would the people close to you say they really know you?
pairs.bText: Do you let people see the unpolished version of you?
pairs.note: You feel truly known, yet the unpolished version of you stays out of view.

pairs.aText: Are you measuring yourself by your own yardstick?
pairs.bText: Are you measuring your life with someone else's ruler?
pairs.note: You measure by your own yardstick, yet someone else's ruler is still in your hand.

pairs.aText: Have you defined success on your own terms?
pairs.bText: Are you comparing your chapter one to someone's chapter ten?
pairs.note: You've defined success your way, yet someone's chapter ten keeps grading your chapter one.

pairs.aText: Are you done arguing with the past?
pairs.bText: Are you holding onto something you should let go?
pairs.note: You're done arguing with the past, yet something of it is still in your hands.

pairs.aText: Have you stopped pressing on a bruise just to check it still hurts?
pairs.bText: Is there a wound you keep reopening?
pairs.note: You've stopped pressing on old bruises, yet one wound keeps finding itself reopened.

pairs.aText: Do you separate your worth from your output?
pairs.bText: Do you like yourself without your achievements?
pairs.note: You separate worth from output, yet without the achievements, liking yourself gets harder.

pairs.aText: Do you believe slow progress is still progress?
pairs.bText: Are you learning at a pace that feels kind?
pairs.note: You believe slow progress counts, yet your own pace doesn't feel kind to you.

pairs.aText: Do you finish what you start?
pairs.bText: Is there a half-finished project waiting patiently for you?
pairs.note: You finish what you start, yet one half-finished project is still waiting patiently.

pairs.aText: Would you bet on your own consistency?
pairs.bText: Did you follow through on what you promised yourself last night?
pairs.note: You'd bet on your own consistency, yet last night's promise to yourself slipped by.

pairs.aText: Have you earned your own trust lately?
pairs.bText: Did you keep a promise to yourself today?
pairs.note: You feel you've earned your own trust, yet today's promise to yourself went unkept.

pairs.aText: Do you know what you actually want right now?
pairs.bText: Have you let yourself want what you actually want?
pairs.note: You know what you want, yet you haven't quite let yourself want it.

pairs.aText: Have you let yourself want something big?
pairs.bText: Are you scared of wanting too much?
pairs.note: You've let yourself want something big, yet wanting too much still scares you.

pairs.aText: Would you tell a friend the goal you're scared to say out loud?
pairs.bText: Is there a creative dream you've never said out loud?
pairs.note: You'd tell a friend the scary goal, yet one creative dream has never left your lips.

pairs.aText: Do you think today's worry will matter in five years?
pairs.bText: Did worry steal a moment from you today?
pairs.note: You know today's worry won't matter in five years, yet it still took a moment from you.

pairs.aText: Would you ask for help if you were stuck?
pairs.bText: Did you ask for help instead of pushing through alone?
pairs.note: You'd ask for help if stuck, you say, yet today you carried it alone.

pairs.aText: Can you be corrected without crumbling?
pairs.bText: Did you let feedback land before defending yourself?
pairs.note: You can take correction, yet today the defense arrived before the feedback landed.

pairs.aText: Are you the same person in every room?
pairs.bText: Is the role you play starting to feel like a costume?
pairs.note: You're the same person in every room, yet the role is starting to feel like a costume.

pairs.aText: Do you let small wins count?
pairs.bText: Is there a milestone you passed without celebrating?
pairs.note: You let small wins count, yet a real milestone passed without its moment.

pairs.aText: Are you tired in a way that rest doesn't reach?
pairs.bText: Have you slept well most nights this week?
pairs.note: The sleep has been good this week, yet a deeper tiredness remains untouched by it.

pairs.aText: Do you like the direction you're quietly heading?
pairs.bText: Are your days adding up to the life you want?
pairs.note: You like the direction you're heading, yet the days don't seem to be adding up to it.

pairs.aText: Do you keep your word even when it's inconvenient?
pairs.bText: Would you cancel plans to watch a new episode the night it drops?
pairs.note: You keep your word even when it costs you, yet a new episode can outbid the plans you made.

pairs.aText: Is your full attention something people can count on?
pairs.bText: Do you watch concerts through your phone screen?
pairs.note: People can count on your full attention, yet at concerts a screen stands between you and the moment.

pairs.aText: Do you listen to understand rather than to reply?
pairs.bText: Did you let someone finish their story without jumping in?
pairs.note: You listen to understand, yet today someone's story didn't get to reach its ending.

pairs.aText: Are you honest when someone asks how you're doing?
pairs.bText: Did you say 'fine' this week when it wasn't true?
pairs.note: You answer honestly when asked how you are, yet 'fine' slipped out this week when it wasn't true.

pairs.aText: Do you trust people until they give you a reason not to?
pairs.bText: Is it easy for you to let new people in?
pairs.note: You offer trust freely, yet the door to really letting someone in stays heavy.

pairs.aText: Do you give without keeping score?
pairs.bText: Would your days feel lighter if you stopped keeping score?
pairs.note: You say you give without counting, yet somewhere a quiet tally is still being kept.

pairs.aText: Are you usually the one who reaches out first?
pairs.bText: Is there a friendship you've been quietly letting fade?
pairs.note: You're the one who reaches out first, yet one friendship is drifting while you watch.

pairs.aText: Have you pretended not to see someone to avoid small talk?
pairs.bText: Would you strike up a chat in a waiting room?
pairs.note: You'd chat with a stranger in a waiting room, yet a familiar face can send you ducking behind a shelf.

pairs.aText: Could you name a friend who knows the real you?
pairs.bText: Would the people close to you say they really know you?
pairs.note: One friend knows the real you, yet the people closest might not say the same.

pairs.aText: Do you let your friends see you on your bad days?
pairs.bText: Do you let people see the unpolished version of you?
pairs.note: Your friends see your bad days, yet the unpolished version of you stays backstage.

pairs.aText: Have you promised yourself just one more episode, then lied?
pairs.bText: Have you earned your own trust lately?
pairs.note: You've earned your own trust, yet 'one more episode' is a promise you break to yourself.

pairs.aText: Would you survive a week without streaming anything?
pairs.bText: Can you sit with yourself without reaching for a distraction?
pairs.note: You can sit with yourself without a distraction, yet a week without streaming feels unsurvivable.

pairs.aText: Would you watch a three-hour movie without checking your phone?
pairs.bText: Could you wait in a long line without checking your phone?
pairs.note: A three-hour movie can hold your whole attention, yet a line is too long to stand in without your phone.

pairs.aText: Do you know how to swim?
pairs.bText: Have you ever swum somewhere you couldn't see the bottom?
pairs.note: You say you can't swim, yet you've floated over water with no visible bottom.

pairs.aText: Are you quick to say it when someone does something well?
pairs.bText: Is there someone whose effort you've noticed but never mentioned?
pairs.note: Praise comes quickly from you, yet one person's effort has been noticed and never named.

pairs.aText: Do you tell people what you like about them while they're around?
pairs.bText: Does your sibling know what you admire about them?
pairs.note: You tell people what you like about them, yet your sibling hasn't heard what you admire.

pairs.aText: Do you find it hard to ask for help?
pairs.bText: Do you offer help before people have to ask?
pairs.note: You offer help before anyone asks, yet asking for it yourself feels heavy.

pairs.aText: Have you stayed in pajamas all day (unapologetically)?
pairs.bText: Can you be still without feeling guilty?
pairs.note: A pajama day needs no apology, yet stillness still hands you a bill of guilt.

pairs.aText: Did you enjoy doing absolutely nothing for a while today?
pairs.bText: Do you rest without guilt when you need to?
pairs.note: Doing nothing felt good today, yet rest and guilt still tend to arrive together.

pairs.aText: Would you drop your plans if a close friend needed you tonight?
pairs.bText: Are you available to everyone except yourself?
pairs.note: You'd drop everything for a friend tonight, yet you're the one person you're rarely available to.

pairs.aText: Do you hide what you actually enjoy to seem cooler?
pairs.bText: Are you the same person in every room?
pairs.note: You're the same person in every room, yet some of what you love stays hidden to look cooler.

pairs.aText: Do you soften your opinions depending on who's listening?
pairs.bText: Do you soften your opinions to keep the peace?
pairs.note: You don't think the audience changes your opinions, yet they soften when peace is on the line.

pairs.aText: Do you speak kindly about people who aren't in the room?
pairs.bText: Would you spoil a twist for someone who annoyed you?
pairs.note: You speak kindly of people who aren't around, yet a spoiler waits for anyone who annoys you.

pairs.aText: Are you patient with people who move slower than you?
pairs.bText: Do you listen at double speed and feel quietly superior?
pairs.note: You're patient with people who move slowly, yet voices get hurried along at double speed.

pairs.aText: Have you ever named a houseplant?
pairs.bText: Can you keep a houseplant alive for more than a year?
pairs.note: Your plants get names, yet they rarely get a second year.

pairs.aText: Do you put on sad songs when you want to feel sad on purpose?
pairs.bText: Do you use stories to avoid feelings you don't want to feel?
pairs.note: You invite sadness in when you choose it, yet stories also help you sidestep the feelings you don't.

pairs.aText: Do you cry more at fiction than at real life?
pairs.bText: Have you cried in front of someone you trust?
pairs.note: Fiction unlocks your tears easily, yet the people you trust haven't seen them.

pairs.aText: Has a TV character ever felt like a friend?
pairs.bText: Have you made a new friend this year?
pairs.note: A character on a screen feels like a friend, yet this year hasn't brought a new one in real life.

pairs.aText: Did an online community ever feel like a hometown?
pairs.bText: Have you shown up to something in your community this month?
pairs.note: An online community feels like a hometown, yet your actual one hasn't seen you this month.

pairs.aText: Are you still connected to where you come from?
pairs.bText: Do you know all four of your grandparents' first names?
pairs.note: You feel connected to where you come from, yet a grandparent's first name is out of reach.

pairs.aText: Have you binged videos of a hobby you'll never actually try?
pairs.bText: Is there a skill you've been meaning to learn?
pairs.note: There's a skill you've been meaning to learn, yet mostly you watch other people doing theirs.

pairs.aText: Did you stay up way too late having fun this week?
pairs.bText: Do you plan rest the way you plan work?
pairs.note: You plan rest like you plan work, yet this week fun rewrote the schedule past midnight.

pairs.aText: Did you make someone laugh today?
pairs.bText: Did you laugh out loud today?
pairs.note: You handed out laughter today, yet none of it seems to have been yours.

pairs.aText: Did you celebrate someone else's win today?
pairs.bText: Is there a milestone you passed without celebrating?
pairs.note: You celebrated someone else's win today, yet one of your own milestones passed without a toast.

pairs.aText: Could you cheer for a friend who got the thing you wanted?
pairs.bText: Can you admit envy when it visits?
pairs.note: You believe you could cheer for a friend who got your dream, yet envy isn't something you can greet by name.

pairs.aText: Could you forgive someone who never said sorry?
pairs.bText: Do you forgive yourself when you let a friend down?
pairs.note: You can forgive someone who never apologized, yet letting a friend down stays unforgiven in you.

pairs.aText: Did you soften when someone snapped at you?
pairs.bText: Did you speak to yourself kindly after a mistake today?
pairs.note: You softened when someone snapped at you, yet your own mistake didn't get the same gentle voice.

pairs.aText: Can you cook a meal without a recipe?
pairs.bText: Can you make rice without burning it?
pairs.note: You can improvise a whole meal, yet rice still wins sometimes.

pairs.aText: Have you ever nailed a parallel park on the first try?
pairs.bText: Could you parallel park on the first try?
pairs.note: You've already nailed it once, yet you doubt you could do it again.

pairs.aText: Can you still do a cartwheel?
pairs.bText: Could you still do a cartwheel?
pairs.note: The same cartwheel got two different answers, depending on which version of you was asked.

pairs.aText: Do you make people feel welcome in your home?
pairs.bText: Do you feel at home with the people around you?
pairs.note: You make people feel at home, yet that feeling doesn't always make it back to you.

pairs.aText: Have you found your people, even just a few?
pairs.bText: Are there people who'd notice if you skipped a week?
pairs.note: You've found your people, yet you're not sure they'd notice a missing week.

pairs.aText: Did you choose rest over one more social yes this week?
pairs.bText: Do you need more alone time than you're getting?
pairs.note: You need more alone time than you're getting, yet one more social yes still beat rest this week.

pairs.aText: Do you save the last episodes because you don't want a show to end?
pairs.bText: Do you finish what you start?
pairs.note: You finish what you start, yet final episodes wait unwatched so the story never has to end.

pairs.aText: Have you heard out an opinion you disagreed with, all the way?
pairs.bText: Have you sought out a new perspective?
pairs.note: You go looking for new perspectives, yet a disagreeing voice didn't get heard all the way through.

pairs.aText: Do you send memes instead of saying you miss someone?
pairs.bText: Is there someone you miss but haven't told?
pairs.note: The memes keep arriving in their inbox, yet the words 'I miss you' stay in drafts.

pairs.aText: Is there someone whose presence you quietly count on?
pairs.bText: Have you thanked someone who's always there?
pairs.note: There's someone whose presence you quietly count on, yet your thanks has stayed quiet too.

pairs.aText: Have you ever made a playlist for someone you cared about?
pairs.bText: Have you told a friend what their friendship means to you?
pairs.note: You've told someone how you feel in songs, yet not what their friendship means in words.

pairs.aText: Would you say the loving thing now instead of waiting?
pairs.bText: Did you say 'I love you' out loud today?
pairs.note: You believe in saying the loving thing now, yet today's 'I love you' stayed unspoken.

pairs.aText: Have you ever binged a podcast's entire back catalog?
pairs.bText: Is there a course, book, or class waiting for your yes?
pairs.note: A whole back catalog got your hours, while the course you want still waits for your yes.

pairs.aText: Do you fall asleep to the same episode every night?
pairs.bText: Did you make time for silence today?
pairs.note: Even falling asleep has a soundtrack, and silence never found a minute of today.

pairs.aText: Have you fallen down a 2 a.m. video rabbit hole?
pairs.bText: Do you rest before you're forced to?
pairs.note: You believe in resting before you're forced to, yet the 2 a.m. rabbit holes know your name.

pairs.aText: Are you keeping a list of everything you've watched?
pairs.bText: Do you keep a record of what you've learned the hard way?
pairs.note: Every show you've watched is carefully logged, yet the lessons you earned the hard way go unrecorded.

pairs.aText: Do you know your Wi-Fi password by heart?
pairs.bText: Do you know anyone's phone number by heart besides your own?
pairs.note: Your memory holds the Wi-Fi password, but no one's phone number besides your own.

pairs.aText: Do you still know your Hogwarts house without checking?
pairs.bText: Can you name all your cousins without pausing?
pairs.note: Your Hogwarts house comes to you instantly, yet naming all your cousins takes a pause.

pairs.aText: Have you done something kind that no one saw?
pairs.bText: Were you kind to yourself in a small way today?
pairs.note: You were kind today where no one could see, except in the one place only you can see.

pairs.aText: Did you ask someone how they're really doing?
pairs.bText: Have you checked in with yourself about how you're really doing?
pairs.note: You asked someone how they're really doing, yet you haven't asked yourself.

pairs.aText: Do you notice when someone's 'I'm fine' isn't?
pairs.bText: Are you honest when someone asks how you're doing?
pairs.note: You can hear when someone else's 'fine' isn't true, yet you hand out your own.

pairs.aText: Have you said sorry when you were wrong?
pairs.bText: Is there an apology you're still hoping to give?
pairs.note: You say sorry when you're wrong, yet one apology is still waiting inside you.

pairs.aText: Have you let a small grudge go this week?
pairs.bText: Have you stopped retelling a story that keeps a hurt alive?
pairs.note: You let a grudge go this week, yet the story that keeps a hurt alive still gets retold.
